core_concept: Personal Hygiene: Keeping the Body Clean Prevents Illness and Helps Others
facts: Handwashing with soap for 20 seconds removes germs that can cause illness; washing without soap or for less time does not remove enough germs to prevent sickness.
Brushing your teeth removes food and bacteria, which prevents cavities; bacteria left on teeth can make holes in them.
Covering a sneeze or cough with your elbow stops germs from spreading to others; sneezing into your hands spreads germs to surfaces you touch.
misconceptions: Some children believe that washing their hands for just a few seconds is enough to get them clean, but germs need 20 seconds of scrubbing with soap to be removed.
